query: What is the standard payment term?

final_response.answer: The standard payment term for invoices is Net 30 days from the invoice issue date.

raw_result.query: What is the standard payment term?

document_output.answer: The standard payment term for invoices is Net 30 days from the invoice issue date.

[Chunk 1 | text | Page 1 | Source payment_terms_policy.pdf]
ERP Payment Terms Policy
Purpose
This policy defines the payment terms, grace periods, discount rules, and late fee policies applied to
vendor and customer invoices in the ERP system. The goal is to ensure predictable financial
operations and consistent vendor relationships.
Standard Payment Terms
The standard payment term for invoices is Net 30 days from the invoice issue date.
Business units may request alternative payment terms for strategic vendors subject to finance
approval.
Payment schedules are automatically enforced through the ERP accounts payable module.
Grace Period

[Chunk 2 | text | Page 2 | Source payment_terms_policy.pdf]
Source: payment_terms_policy.pdf | Page: 2 | Type: text

Certain strategic vendors may negotiate custom payment terms such as Net 45 or Net 60.
Such agreements must be recorded in the vendor master record and approved by the finance
department.

[Chunk 3 | text | Page 1 | Source payment_terms_policy.pdf]
Source: payment_terms_policy.pdf | Page: 1 | Type: text | Section: ERP Payment Terms Policy

[Chunk 4 | text | Page 1 | Source payment_terms_policy.pdf]
Suppliers may offer early payment discounts, commonly expressed as 2/10 Net 30.
This indicates that a 2 percent discount is available if payment is made within 10 days.
The ERP system automatically identifies eligible discounts during invoice processing.
Exceptions and Special Agreements

[Chunk 5 | text | Page 1 | Source payment_terms_policy.pdf]
Payment schedules are automatically enforced through the ERP accounts payable module.
Grace Period
A grace period of up to 5 calendar days after the invoice due date may be applied before late
penalties are enforced.
This grace period accounts for banking delays, public holidays, and cross-border transactions.
Late Payment Fees
Invoices unpaid after the grace period may incur a late fee of 2% of the outstanding invoice amount.
Recurring overdue payments may trigger vendor review and procurement escalation.
Early Payment Discounts
Suppliers may offer early payment discounts, commonly expressed as 2/10 Net 30.

[Chunk 6 | text | Page 1 | Source invoice_matching_policy.pdf]
Invoice Matching Policy
Purpose
This policy defines invoice matching procedures used by the ERP accounts payable module.
three-way Matching
Invoices are validated using a three-way match between purchase orders, goods receipts, and
supplier invoices.
This process ensures that invoices correspond to approved procurement transactions.
Tolerance Rules
A price variance tolerance of up to 2 percent may be allowed between the invoice and the purchase
order.
Quantity variances may be tolerated up to 5 percent depending on product category.
Dispute Resolution
Invoices failing the matching process are flagged for review by the accounts payable team.

[Chunk 7 | text | Page 1 | Source purchase_order_approval_policy.pdf]
Source: purchase_order_approval_policy.pdf | Page: 1 | Type: text | Section: Purchase Order Approval Policy

[Chunk 8 | text | Page 1 | Source invoice_matching_policy.pdf]
Source: invoice_matching_policy.pdf | Page: 1 | Type: text | Section: Invoice Matching Policy

Purchase Order Approval Policy
Purpose
This document defines the purchase order approval process used within the ERP procurement
module.
Purchase Order Thresholds
Purchase orders below 5,000 EUR may be approved by department managers.
Purchase orders between 5,000 EUR and 25,000 EUR require procurement manager approval.
Purchase orders above 25,000 EUR require director-level approval.
Purchase Order Lifecycle
The ERP system manages the full purchase order lifecycle including creation, approval, supplier
confirmation, delivery tracking, and invoice matching.
high-value Procurement Controls

[Chunk 5 | text | Page 1 | Source vendor_onboarding_policy.pdf]
Vendor Onboarding Policy
Purpose
This document defines the standard procedure for onboarding new vendors into the ERP
procurement system.
Required Vendor Documentation
Vendors must submit a completed vendor registration form.
Tax identification documents must be provided for regulatory compliance.
Bank account details must be verified to prevent fraudulent payments.
Compliance certifications such as ISO or regulatory licenses may be required depending on vendor
category.
Vendor Approval Workflow
Vendor onboarding follows a three-stage approval workflow: procurement review, finance
validation, and compliance verification.

[Chunk 6 | text | Page 1 | Source procurement_guidelines.pdf]
Procurement Governance Guidelines
Purpose
This document outlines procurement governance, supplier selection procedures, and compliance
expectations within the ERP system.
Supplier Selection
Procurement teams must evaluate suppliers based on cost, quality, delivery reliability, and
compliance certifications.
Competitive bidding is required for high-value procurements.
Request for Quotation Process
For purchases exceeding internal thresholds, a Request for Quotation (RFQ) must be issued to
multiple suppliers.
The ERP procurement module records RFQ responses for evaluation.
Contract Management

final_response.reasoning: The document "purchase_order_approval_policy.pdf" (Chunk 1) outlines the purchase order approval process. [Chunk 1 | text | Page 1 | Source purchase_order_approval_policy.pdf]
Purchase Order Approval Policy
Purpose
This document defines the purchase order approval process used within the ERP procurement
module.
Purchase Order Thresholds
Purchase orders below 5,000 EUR may be approved by department managers.
Purchase orders between 5,000 EUR and 25,000 EUR require procurement manager approval.
Purchase orders above 25,000 EUR require director-level approval.
Purchase Order Lifecycle
The ERP system manages the full purchase order lifecycle including creation, approval, supplier
confirmation, delivery tracking, and invoice matching.
high-value Procurement Controls

[Chunk 2 | text | Page 1 | Source purchase_order_approval_policy.pdf]

[Chunk 3 | text | Page 1 | Source purchase_order_approval_policy.pdf]
confirmation, delivery tracking, and invoice matching.
high-value Procurement Controls
high-value purchases require competitive supplier quotations.
Procurement must verify budget availability before issuing the purchase order.
Audit and Compliance
All purchase orders are logged in the ERP system and subject to internal audit review.

[Chunk 4 | text | Page 1 | Source invoice_matching_policy.pdf]
Invoice Matching Policy
Purpose
This policy defines invoice matching procedures used by the ERP accounts payable module.
three-way Matching
Invoices are validated using a three-way match between purchase orders, goods receipts, and
supplier invoices.
This process ensures that invoices correspond to approved procurement transactions.
Tolerance Rules
A price variance tolerance of up to 2 percent may be allowed between the invoice and the purchase
order.
Quantity variances may be tolerated up to 5 percent depending on product category.
Dispute Resolution
Invoices failing the matching process are flagged for review by the accounts payable team.

[Chunk 5 | text | Page 1 | Source vendor_onboarding_policy.pdf]
Vendor Onboarding Policy
Purpose
This document defines the standard procedure for onboarding new vendors into the ERP
procurement system.
Required Vendor Documentation
Vendors must submit a completed vendor registration form.
Tax identification documents must be provided for regulatory compliance.
Bank account details must be verified to prevent fraudulent payments.
Compliance certifications such as ISO or regulatory licenses may be required depending on vendor
category.
Vendor Approval Workflow
Vendor onboarding follows a three-stage approval workflow: procurement review, finance
validation, and compliance verification.

department.

Procurement Governance Guidelines
Purpose
This document outlines procurement governance, supplier selection procedures, and compliance
expectations within the ERP system.
Supplier Selection
Procurement teams must evaluate suppliers based on cost, quality, delivery reliability, and
compliance certifications.
Competitive bidding is required for high-value procurements.
Request for Quotation Process
For purchases exceeding internal thresholds, a Request for Quotation (RFQ) must be issued to
multiple suppliers.
The ERP procurement module records RFQ responses for evaluation.
Contract Management

ERP Payment Terms Policy
Purpose
This policy defines the payment terms, grace periods, discount rules, and late fee policies applied to

Standard Payment Terms
The standard payment term for invoices is Net 30 days from the invoice issue date.

Payment schedules are automatically enforced through the ERP accounts payable module.

Purpose
This policy defines the payment terms, grace periods, discount rules, and late fee policies applied to

Standard Payment Terms

approval.
Payment schedules are automatically enforced through the ERP accounts payable module.
Grace Period

Grace Period

penalties are enforced.
This grace period accounts for banking delays, public holidays, and cross-border transactions.
Late Payment Fees
Invoices unpaid after the grace period may incur a late fee of 2% of the outstanding invoice amount.
Recurring overdue payments may trigger vendor review and procurement escalation.
Early Payment Discounts
Suppliers may offer early payment discounts, commonly expressed as 2/10 Net 30.

query: What procurement rules apply to high value purchases?

3. A Request for Quotation (RFQ) must be issued to multiple suppliers for purchases exceeding internal thresholds.
4. The ERP procurement module records RFQ responses for evaluation.
5. High-value purchases require director-level approval for purchase orders above 25,000 EUR.

query: Explain how the ERP system handles payment terms and discounts.

final_response.answer:
The ERP system handles payment terms and discounts as follows:

1. **Standard Payment Terms**: The standard payment term for invoices is Net 30 days from the invoice issue date. This means that invoices are due for payment 30 days after the date they were issued.

2. **Alternative Payment Terms**: Business units may request alternative payment terms for strategic vendors, subject to finance approval. This allows for customized payment terms for specific vendors.

3. **Payment Schedules**: Payment schedules are automatically enforced through the ERP accounts payable module. This ensures that invoices are paid according to the agreed-upon payment terms.

4. **Early Payment Discounts**: Suppliers may offer early payment discounts, commonly expressed as 2/10 Net 30. This indicates that a 2 percent discount is available if payment is made within 10 days. The ERP system automatically identifies eligible discounts during invoice processing.

5. **Grace Period**: A grace period of up to 5 calendar days after the invoice due date may be applied before late penalties are enforced. This accounts for banking delays, public holidays, and cross-border transactions.

6. **Late Payment Fees**: Invoices unpaid after the grace period may incur a late fee of 2% of the outstanding invoice amount. Recurring overdue payments may trigger vendor review and procurement escalation.

The ERP system's automatic payment term enforcement and discount identification ensure that payment terms and discounts are consistently applied and tracked throughout the payment process.

query: Explain the three way matching process for invoices.

final_response.answer:
According to the Invoice Matching Policy, the three-way matching process for invoices involves a validation of the invoice using a match between the following three components:

1. Purchase Orders
2. Goods Receipts
3. Supplier Invoices

This process ensures that the invoice corresponds to an approved procurement transaction. The three-way match helps to prevent incorrect or fraudulent invoices from being paid.

query: Explain how procurement ensures supplier compliance.

final_response.answer:
Supplier compliance is ensured through the following procedures:

1. **Compliance certifications**: Suppliers must provide compliance certifications such as ISO or regulatory licenses, depending on the vendor category.
2. **Procurement review**: During the vendor onboarding process, procurement reviews the vendor's documentation and certifications to ensure compliance.
3. **Compliance verification**: The compliance verification stage of the vendor approval workflow ensures that vendors meet the required compliance standards.
4. **Internal audit review**: All purchase orders are logged in the ERP system and subject to internal audit review to ensure compliance with company policies and procedures.
5. **Supplier evaluation**: Procurement teams evaluate suppliers based on cost, quality, delivery reliability, and compliance certifications, which helps to identify non-compliant suppliers.

By following these procedures, procurement ensures that suppliers meet the required compliance standards and reduces the risk of non-compliance.

query: Describe how purchase orders are approved in the ERP system.

final_response.answer:
According to the Purchase Order Approval Policy, purchase orders are approved in the ERP system based on the following thresholds:

- Purchase orders below 5,000 EUR may be approved by department managers.
- Purchase orders between 5,000 EUR and 25,000 EUR require procurement manager approval.
- Purchase orders above 25,000 EUR require director-level approval.

The ERP system manages the full purchase order lifecycle, including creation, approval, supplier confirmation, delivery tracking, and invoice matching.

query: What are the financial controls used in procurement?

final_response.answer:
Based on the provided context, the financial controls used in procurement include:

1. Verification of budget availability before issuing the purchase order.
2. Purchase order thresholds for approval:
   - Purchase orders below 5,000 EUR may be approved by department managers.
   - Purchase orders between 5,000 EUR and 25,000 EUR require procurement manager approval.
   - Purchase orders above 25,000 EUR require director-level approval.
3. Dual approval for changes to vendor bank details.
4. Automatic tracking of approval status and audit logs by the ERP system.
5. Recording of custom payment terms for strategic vendors in the vendor master record and approval by the finance department.

query: Explain how supplier selection decisions are made.

final_response.answer:
Supplier selection decisions are made by evaluating suppliers based on the following criteria:

1. Cost
2. Quality
3. Delivery reliability
4. Compliance certifications

This evaluation process is outlined in the Procurement Governance Guidelines document, which states that procurement teams must assess suppliers based on these factors to ensure that the best supplier is selected for the procurement.

Additionally, for high-value procurements, competitive bidding is required, and a Request for Quotation (RFQ) must be issued to multiple suppliers. The ERP procurement module records RFQ responses for evaluation, allowing procurement teams to compare and select the best supplier.

query: Which customers made the highest payments and what is the standard payment term policy?

final_response.answer: The top 10 customers who made the highest payments are Heidi Hill, Paul Brown, Denise Johnson, Mark Thomas, Carly Phillips, Olivia Long, Paul Chen, Richard Cummings, James Horton, and Samantha Shannon. The standard payment term policy is Net 30 days from the invoice issue date.
final_response.reasoning: Based on the Database Results, the top 10 customers who made the highest payments are listed in descending order of total payment amount. The Document Evidence, specifically Chunk 1 from payment_terms_policy.pdf, states that the standard payment term for invoices is Net 30 days from the invoice issue date. This policy is applicable to all customers unless alternative payment terms have been negotiated and approved by the finance department.

[Chunk 1 | text | Page 1 | Source payment_terms_policy.pdf]
ERP Payment Terms Policy
Purpose
This policy defines the payment terms, grace periods, discount rules, and late fee policies applied to
vendor and customer invoices in the ERP system. The goal is to ensure predictable financial
operations and consistent vendor relationships.
Standard Payment Terms
The standard payment term for invoices is Net 30 days from the invoice issue date.
Business units may request alternative payment terms for strategic vendors subject to finance
approval.
Payment schedules are automatically enforced through the ERP accounts payable module.
Grace Period

[Chunk 2 | text | Page 2 | Source payment_terms_policy.pdf]
Source: payment_terms_policy.pdf | Page: 2 | Type: text

Certain strategic vendors may negotiate custom payment terms such as Net 45 or Net 60.
Such agreements must be recorded in the vendor master record and approved by the finance
department.

[Chunk 3 | text | Page 1 | Source payment_terms_policy.pdf]
Source: payment_terms_policy.pdf | Page: 1 | Type: text | Section: ERP Payment Terms Policy

[Chunk 4 | text | Page 1 | Source invoice_matching_policy.pdf]
Source: invoice_matching_policy.pdf | Page: 1 | Type: text | Section: Invoice Matching Policy

[Chunk 5 | text | Page 1 | Source payment_terms_policy.pdf]
Suppliers may offer early payment discounts, commonly expressed as 2/10 Net 30.
This indicates that a 2 percent discount is available if payment is made within 10 days.
The ERP system automatically identifies eligible discounts during invoice processing.
Exceptions and Special Agreements

[Chunk 6 | text | Page 1 | Source invoice_matching_policy.pdf]
Invoice Matching Policy
Purpose
This policy defines invoice matching procedures used by the ERP accounts payable module.
three-way Matching
Invoices are validated using a three-way match between purchase orders, goods receipts, and
supplier invoices.
This process ensures that invoices correspond to approved procurement transactions.
Tolerance Rules
A price variance tolerance of up to 2 percent may be allowed between the invoice and the purchase
order.
Quantity variances may be tolerated up to 5 percent depending on product category.
Dispute Resolution
Invoices failing the matching process are flagged for review by the accounts payable team.

[Chunk 7 | text | Page 1 | Source purchase_order_approval_policy.pdf]
Purchase Order Approval Policy
Purpose
This document defines the purchase order approval process used within the ERP procurement
module.
Purchase Order Thresholds
Purchase orders below 5,000 EUR may be approved by department managers.
Purchase orders between 5,000 EUR and 25,000 EUR require procurement manager approval.
Purchase orders above 25,000 EUR require director-level approval.
Purchase Order Lifecycle
The ERP system manages the full purchase order lifecycle including creation, approval, supplier
confirmation, delivery tracking, and invoice matching.
high-value Procurement Controls

[Chunk 8 | text | Page 1 | Source payment_terms_policy.pdf]
Payment schedules are automatically enforced through the ERP accounts payable module.
Grace Period
A grace period of up to 5 calendar days after the invoice due date may be applied before late
penalties are enforced.
This grace period accounts for banking delays, public holidays, and cross-border transactions.
Late Payment Fees
Invoices unpaid after the grace period may incur a late fee of 2% of the outstanding invoice amount.
Recurring overdue payments may trigger vendor review and procurement escalation.
Early Payment Discounts
Suppliers may offer early payment discounts, commonly expressed as 2/10 Net 30.

[Chunk 9 | text | Page 1 | Source purchase_order_approval_policy.pdf]
Source: purchase_order_approval_policy.pdf | Page: 1 | Type: text | Section: Purchase Order Approval Policy

[Chunk 10 | text | Page 1 | Source purchase_order_approval_policy.pdf]
confirmation, delivery tracking, and invoice matching.
high-value Procurement Controls
high-value purchases require competitive supplier quotations.
Procurement must verify budget availability before issuing the purchase order.
Audit and Compliance
All purchase orders are logged in the ERP system and subject to internal audit review.

Invoices are validated using a three-way match between purchase orders, goods receipts, and
supplier invoices.
This process ensures that invoices correspond to approved procurement transactions.
Tolerance Rules
A price variance tolerance of up to 2 percent may be allowed between the invoice and the purchase
order.
Quantity variances may be tolerated up to 5 percent depending on product category.
Dispute Resolution
Invoices failing the matching process are flagged for review by the accounts payable team.

[Chunk 7 | text | Page 1 | Source purchase_order_approval_policy.pdf]
Purchase Order Approval Policy
Purpose
This document defines the purchase order approval process used within the ERP procurement
module.
Purchase Order Thresholds
Purchase orders below 5,000 EUR may be approved by department managers.
Purchase orders between 5,000 EUR and 25,000 EUR require procurement manager approval.
Purchase orders above 25,000 EUR require director-level approval.
Purchase Order Lifecycle
The ERP system manages the full purchase order lifecycle including creation, approval, supplier
confirmation, delivery tracking, and invoice matching.
high-value Procurement Controls

Grace Period
A grace period of up to 5 calendar days after the invoice due date may be applied before late
penalties are enforced.
This grace period accounts for banking delays, public holidays, and cross-border transactions.
Late Payment Fees
Invoices unpaid after the grace period may incur a late fee of 2% of the outstanding invoice amount.
Recurring overdue payments may trigger vendor review and procurement escalation.
Early Payment Discounts
Suppliers may offer early payment discounts, commonly expressed as 2/10 Net 30.

Purpose

three-way Matching

order.

query: Which countries generate the highest sales and what procurement controls apply to suppliers?

final_response.answer: The top 10 countries by sales are: US, GB, IN, BR, DE, FR, MX, AU, CA, and ES. Procurement controls for high-value purchases include competitive supplier quotations, verification of budget availability, and director-level approval. Suppliers must submit a completed vendor registration form, tax identification documents, and bank account details for onboarding. Procurement teams must evaluate suppliers based on cost, quality, delivery reliability, and compliance certifications.
final_response.reasoning: Based on the Database Results, the top 10 countries by sales are listed in descending order. Document Evidence from procurement_guidelines.pdf and purchase_order_approval_policy.pdf outlines procurement controls for high-value purchases, including competitive supplier quotations, verification of budget availability, and director-level approval. Vendor onboarding policy requires suppliers to submit a completed vendor registration form, tax identification documents, and bank account details. Supplier selection procedures also require evaluation based on cost, quality, delivery reliability, and compliance certifications.
final_response.sql_query: SELECT c.country, SUM(o.total_usd) AS sales FROM customers c JOIN orders o ON c.customer_id = o.customer_id GROUP BY c.country ORDER BY sales DESC LIMIT 10

[Chunk 1 | text | Page 1 | Source invoice_matching_policy.pdf]
Invoice Matching Policy
Purpose
This policy defines invoice matching procedures used by the ERP accounts payable module.
three-way Matching
Invoices are validated using a three-way match between purchase orders, goods receipts, and
supplier invoices.
This process ensures that invoices correspond to approved procurement transactions.
Tolerance Rules
A price variance tolerance of up to 2 percent may be allowed between the invoice and the purchase
order.
Quantity variances may be tolerated up to 5 percent depending on product category.
Dispute Resolution
Invoices failing the matching process are flagged for review by the accounts payable team.

[Chunk 2 | text | Page 1 | Source purchase_order_approval_policy.pdf]
confirmation, delivery tracking, and invoice matching.
high-value Procurement Controls
high-value purchases require competitive supplier quotations.
Procurement must verify budget availability before issuing the purchase order.
Audit and Compliance
All purchase orders are logged in the ERP system and subject to internal audit review.

[Chunk 3 | text | Page 1 | Source invoice_matching_policy.pdf]
Source: invoice_matching_policy.pdf | Page: 1 | Type: text | Section: Invoice Matching Policy

[Chunk 4 | text | Page 1 | Source payment_terms_policy.pdf]
Suppliers may offer early payment discounts, commonly expressed as 2/10 Net 30.
This indicates that a 2 percent discount is available if payment is made within 10 days.
The ERP system automatically identifies eligible discounts during invoice processing.
Exceptions and Special Agreements

[Chunk 5 | text | Page 1 | Source payment_terms_policy.pdf]
ERP Payment Terms Policy
Purpose
This policy defines the payment terms, grace periods, discount rules, and late fee policies applied to
vendor and customer invoices in the ERP system. The goal is to ensure predictable financial
operations and consistent vendor relationships.
Standard Payment Terms
The standard payment term for invoices is Net 30 days from the invoice issue date.
Business units may request alternative payment terms for strategic vendors subject to finance
approval.
Payment schedules are automatically enforced through the ERP accounts payable module.
Grace Period

[Chunk 6 | text | Page 1 | Source payment_terms_policy.pdf]
Payment schedules are automatically enforced through the ERP accounts payable module.
Grace Period
A grace period of up to 5 calendar days after the invoice due date may be applied before late
penalties are enforced.
This grace period accounts for banking delays, public holidays, and cross-border transactions.
Late Payment Fees
Invoices unpaid after the grace period may incur a late fee of 2% of the outstanding invoice amount.
Recurring overdue payments may trigger vendor review and procurement escalation.
Early Payment Discounts
Suppliers may offer early payment discounts, commonly expressed as 2/10 Net 30.

[Chunk 7 | text | Page 1 | Source invoice_matching_policy.pdf]
Invoices failing the matching process are flagged for review by the accounts payable team.
Disputed invoices may be returned to the vendor for correction.
Automated Controls
The ERP system automatically flags mismatches exceeding tolerance thresholds and prevents
payment until resolved.

[Chunk 8 | text | Page 1 | Source purchase_order_approval_policy.pdf]
Purchase Order Approval Policy
Purpose
This document defines the purchase order approval process used within the ERP procurement
module.
Purchase Order Thresholds
Purchase orders below 5,000 EUR may be approved by department managers.
Purchase orders between 5,000 EUR and 25,000 EUR require procurement manager approval.
Purchase orders above 25,000 EUR require director-level approval.
Purchase Order Lifecycle
The ERP system manages the full purchase order lifecycle including creation, approval, supplier
confirmation, delivery tracking, and invoice matching.
high-value Procurement Controls

[Chunk 9 | text | Page 1 | Source procurement_guidelines.pdf]
Procurement Governance Guidelines
Purpose
This document outlines procurement governance, supplier selection procedures, and compliance
expectations within the ERP system.
Supplier Selection
Procurement teams must evaluate suppliers based on cost, quality, delivery reliability, and
compliance certifications.
Competitive bidding is required for high-value procurements.
Request for Quotation Process
For purchases exceeding internal thresholds, a Request for Quotation (RFQ) must be issued to
multiple suppliers.
The ERP procurement module records RFQ responses for evaluation.
Contract Management

[Chunk 10 | text | Page 1 | Source purchase_order_approval_policy.pdf]

[Chunk 1 | text | Page 1 | Source payment_terms_policy.pdf]
ERP Payment Terms Policy
Purpose
This policy defines the payment terms, grace periods, discount rules, and late fee policies applied to
vendor and customer invoices in the ERP system. The goal is to ensure predictable financial
operations and consistent vendor relationships.
Standard Payment Terms
The standard payment term for invoices is Net 30 days from the invoice issue date.
Business units may request alternative payment terms for strategic vendors subject to finance
approval.
Payment schedules are automatically enforced through the ERP accounts payable module.
Grace Period

[Chunk 2 | text | Page 1 | Source payment_terms_policy.pdf]
Payment schedules are automatically enforced through the ERP accounts payable module.
Grace Period
A grace period of up to 5 calendar days after the invoice due date may be applied before late
penalties are enforced.
This grace period accounts for banking delays, public holidays, and cross-border transactions.
Late Payment Fees
Invoices unpaid after the grace period may incur a late fee of 2% of the outstanding invoice amount.
Recurring overdue payments may trigger vendor review and procurement escalation.
Early Payment Discounts
Suppliers may offer early payment discounts, commonly expressed as 2/10 Net 30.

[Chunk 3 | text | Page 1 | Source payment_terms_policy.pdf]
Source: payment_terms_policy.pdf | Page: 1 | Type: text | Section: ERP Payment Terms Policy

[Chunk 4 | text | Page 2 | Source payment_terms_policy.pdf]
Source: payment_terms_policy.pdf | Page: 2 | Type: text

Certain strategic vendors may negotiate custom payment terms such as Net 45 or Net 60.
Such agreements must be recorded in the vendor master record and approved by the finance
department.

[Chunk 5 | text | Page 1 | Source payment_terms_policy.pdf]
Suppliers may offer early payment discounts, commonly expressed as 2/10 Net 30.
This indicates that a 2 percent discount is available if payment is made within 10 days.
The ERP system automatically identifies eligible discounts during invoice processing.
Exceptions and Special Agreements

[Chunk 6 | text | Page 1 | Source invoice_matching_policy.pdf]
Source: invoice_matching_policy.pdf | Page: 1 | Type: text | Section: Invoice Matching Policy

[Chunk 7 | text | Page 1 | Source invoice_matching_policy.pdf]
Invoice Matching Policy
Purpose
This policy defines invoice matching procedures used by the ERP accounts payable module.
three-way Matching
Invoices are validated using a three-way match between purchase orders, goods receipts, and
supplier invoices.
This process ensures that invoices correspond to approved procurement transactions.
Tolerance Rules
A price variance tolerance of up to 2 percent may be allowed between the invoice and the purchase
order.
Quantity variances may be tolerated up to 5 percent depending on product category.
Dispute Resolution
Invoices failing the matching process are flagged for review by the accounts payable team.

Purchase Order Approval Policy
Purpose
This document defines the purchase order approval process used within the ERP procurement
module.
Purchase Order Thresholds
Purchase orders below 5,000 EUR may be approved by department managers.
Purchase orders between 5,000 EUR and 25,000 EUR require procurement manager approval.
Purchase orders above 25,000 EUR require director-level approval.
Purchase Order Lifecycle
The ERP system manages the full purchase order lifecycle including creation, approval, supplier
confirmation, delivery tracking, and invoice matching.
high-value Procurement Controls

confirmation, delivery tracking, and invoice matching.
high-value Procurement Controls

Audit and Compliance
All purchase orders are logged in the ERP system and subject to internal audit review.

[Chunk 4 | text | Page 1 | Source payment_terms_policy.pdf]

Procurement Governance Guidelines
Purpose
This document outlines procurement governance, supplier selection procedures, and compliance
expectations within the ERP system.
Supplier Selection
Procurement teams must evaluate suppliers based on cost, quality, delivery reliability, and
compliance certifications.
Competitive bidding is required for high-value procurements.
Request for Quotation Process
For purchases exceeding internal thresholds, a Request for Quotation (RFQ) must be issued to
multiple suppliers.
The ERP procurement module records RFQ responses for evaluation.
Contract Management

validation, and compliance verification.

high-risk vendors may require additional approval from senior procurement leadership.

Changes to vendor bank details require dual approval.

[Chunk 7 | text | Page 1 | Source invoice_matching_policy.pdf]
Invoice Matching Policy
Purpose
This policy defines invoice matching procedures used by the ERP accounts payable module.
three-way Matching
Invoices are validated using a three-way match between purchase orders, goods receipts, and
supplier invoices.
This process ensures that invoices correspond to approved procurement transactions.
Tolerance Rules
A price variance tolerance of up to 2 percent may be allowed between the invoice and the purchase
order.
Quantity variances may be tolerated up to 5 percent depending on product category.
Dispute Resolution
Invoices failing the matching process are flagged for review by the accounts payable team.

Vendor Onboarding Policy
Purpose
This document defines the standard procedure for onboarding new vendors into the ERP
procurement system.
Required Vendor Documentation
Vendors must submit a completed vendor registration form.
Tax identification documents must be provided for regulatory compliance.
Bank account details must be verified to prevent fraudulent payments.
Compliance certifications such as ISO or regulatory licenses may be required depending on vendor
category.
Vendor Approval Workflow
Vendor onboarding follows a three-stage approval workflow: procurement review, finance
validation, and compliance verification.

Late Payment Fees

The invoice dispute process is as follows:
Invoices failing the three-way match between purchase orders, goods receipts, and supplier invoices are flagged for review by the accounts payable team. Disputed invoices may be returned to the vendor for correction. Automated controls in the ERP system flag mismatches exceeding tolerance thresholds and prevent payment until resolved.

The ERP system also has automated controls that flag mismatches exceeding tolerance thresholds and prevent payment until resolved.

Invoice Matching Policy
Purpose
This policy defines invoice matching procedures used by the ERP accounts payable module.
three-way Matching
Invoices are validated using a three-way match between purchase orders, goods receipts, and
supplier invoices.
This process ensures that invoices correspond to approved procurement transactions.
Tolerance Rules
A price variance tolerance of up to 2 percent may be allowed between the invoice and the purchase
order.
Quantity variances may be tolerated up to 5 percent depending on product category.
Dispute Resolution
Invoices failing the matching process are flagged for review by the accounts payable team.

confirmation, delivery tracking, and invoice matching.

[Chunk 3 | text | Page 1 | Source invoice_matching_policy.pdf]
Invoices failing the matching process are flagged for review by the accounts payable team.

multiple suppliers.

Contract Management

Payment schedules are automatically enforced through the ERP accounts payable module.
Grace Period
A grace period of up to 5 calendar days after the invoice due date may be applied before late
penalties are enforced.
This grace period accounts for banking delays, public holidays, and cross-border transactions.
Late Payment Fees
Invoices unpaid after the grace period may incur a late fee of 2% of the outstanding invoice amount.
Recurring overdue payments may trigger vendor review and procurement escalation.
Early Payment Discounts
Suppliers may offer early payment discounts, commonly expressed as 2/10 Net 30.

approval.

final_response.reasoning: The top 10 customers generating the highest revenue are determined by the database results, which show the revenue generated by each customer in descending order. The required vendor documents for onboarding are specified in the vendor onboarding policy document.
final_response.sql_query: SELECT c.name, SUM(o.total_usd) AS revenue FROM customers c JOIN orders o ON c.customer_id = o.customer_id GROUP BY c.customer_id ORDER BY revenue DESC LIMIT 10

Purchase Order Approval Policy
Purpose
This document defines the purchase order approval process used within the ERP procurement
module.
Purchase Order Thresholds

Purchase Order Lifecycle

confirmation, delivery tracking, and invoice matching.

Exceptions and Special Agreements

[Chunk 10 | text | Page 1 | Source invoice_matching_policy.pdf]
Invoice Matching Policy
Purpose
This policy defines invoice matching procedures used by the ERP accounts payable module.
three-way Matching
Invoices are validated using a three-way match between purchase orders, goods receipts, and
supplier invoices.
This process ensures that invoices correspond to approved procurement transactions.
Tolerance Rules
A price variance tolerance of up to 2 percent may be allowed between the invoice and the purchase
order.
Quantity variances may be tolerated up to 5 percent depending on product category.
Dispute Resolution
Invoices failing the matching process are flagged for review by the accounts payable team.

three-way Matching

order.

This policy defines invoice matching procedures used by the ERP accounts payable module.
three-way Matching
Invoices are validated using a three-way match between purchase orders, goods receipts, and
supplier invoices.
This process ensures that invoices correspond to approved procurement transactions.
Tolerance Rules
A price variance tolerance of up to 2 percent may be allowed between the invoice and the purchase
order.
Quantity variances may be tolerated up to 5 percent depending on product category.
Dispute Resolution
Invoices failing the matching process are flagged for review by the accounts payable team.

Purpose
This policy defines the payment terms, grace periods, discount rules, and late fee policies applied to

operations and consistent vendor relationships.
Standard Payment Terms

[Chunk 9 | text | Page 1 | Source payment_terms_policy.pdf]

Late Payment Fees
Invoices unpaid after the grace period may incur a late fee of 2% of the outstanding invoice amount.

Early Payment Discounts

Vendor Onboarding Policy
Purpose
This document defines the standard procedure for onboarding new vendors into the ERP
procurement system.
Required Vendor Documentation
Vendors must submit a completed vendor registration form.
Tax identification documents must be provided for regulatory compliance.
Bank account details must be verified to prevent fraudulent payments.
Compliance certifications such as ISO or regulatory licenses may be required depending on vendor
category.
Vendor Approval Workflow
Vendor onboarding follows a three-stage approval workflow: procurement review, finance
validation, and compliance verification.

[Chunk 3 | text | Page 1 | Source vendor_onboarding_policy.pdf]
validation, and compliance verification.
The ERP system automatically tracks approval status and audit logs.
Vendor Risk Assessment
Vendors are assigned a credit rating or risk category.
high-risk vendors may require additional approval from senior procurement leadership.
Vendor Master Data Management
All vendor information is stored in the vendor master table of the ERP system.
Changes to vendor bank details require dual approval.

[Chunk 4 | text | Page 1 | Source purchase_order_approval_policy.pdf]
Purchase Order Approval Policy
Purpose
This document defines the purchase order approval process used within the ERP procurement
module.
Purchase Order Thresholds
Purchase orders below 5,000 EUR may be approved by department managers.
Purchase orders between 5,000 EUR and 25,000 EUR require procurement manager approval.
Purchase orders above 25,000 EUR require director-level approval.
Purchase Order Lifecycle
The ERP system manages the full purchase order lifecycle including creation, approval, supplier
confirmation, delivery tracking, and invoice matching.
high-value Procurement Controls

[Chunk 5 | text | Page 1 | Source procurement_guidelines.pdf]
The ERP procurement module records RFQ responses for evaluation.
Contract Management
long-term supplier agreements must be documented within the ERP contract management
module.
Contracts should define pricing, service levels, and termination clauses.
Compliance and Ethics
All procurement activities must comply with company ethical standards and anti-corruption
policies.
Employees involved in procurement decisions must disclose conflicts of interest.

[Chunk 6 | text | Page 1 | Source purchase_order_approval_policy.pdf]
confirmation, delivery tracking, and invoice matching.
high-value Procurement Controls
high-value purchases require competitive supplier quotations.
Procurement must verify budget availability before issuing the purchase order.
Audit and Compliance
All purchase orders are logged in the ERP system and subject to internal audit review.

Procurement Governance Guidelines
Purpose
This document outlines procurement governance, supplier selection procedures, and compliance
expectations within the ERP system.
Supplier Selection
Procurement teams must evaluate suppliers based on cost, quality, delivery reliability, and
compliance certifications.
Competitive bidding is required for high-value procurements.
Request for Quotation Process
For purchases exceeding internal thresholds, a Request for Quotation (RFQ) must be issued to
multiple suppliers.
The ERP procurement module records RFQ responses for evaluation.
Contract Management

[Chunk 9 | text | Page 1 | Source purchase_order_approval_policy.pdf]
Source: purchase_order_approval_policy.pdf | Page: 1 | Type: text | Section: Purchase Order Approval Policy

[Chunk 10 | text | Page 1 | Source payment_terms_policy.pdf]
Payment schedules are automatically enforced through the ERP accounts payable module.
Grace Period
A grace period of up to 5 calendar days after the invoice due date may be applied before late
penalties are enforced.
This grace period accounts for banking delays, public holidays, and cross-border transactions.
Late Payment Fees
Invoices unpaid after the grace period may incur a late fee of 2% of the outstanding invoice amount.
Recurring overdue payments may trigger vendor review and procurement escalation.
Early Payment Discounts
Suppliers may offer early payment discounts, commonly expressed as 2/10 Net 30.

Suppliers may offer early payment discounts, commonly expressed as 2/10 Net 30.

The ERP system automatically identifies eligible discounts during invoice processing.
Exceptions and Special Agreements

Source: payment_terms_policy.pdf | Page: 2 | Type: text

Certain strategic vendors may negotiate custom payment terms such as Net 45 or Net 60.

department.

query: Which product categories generate the most revenue and what procurement rules apply?

final_response.answer: The top 3 product categories generating the most revenue are Home & Kitchen, Sports, and Fashion. For high-value procurements, competitive supplier quotations are required, and procurement must verify budget availability before issuing the purchase order. Purchase orders above 25,000 EUR require director-level approval.
final_response.reasoning:
Based on the Database Results, the top 3 product categories generating the most revenue are Home & Kitchen, Sports, and Fashion. 

For procurement rules, we refer to the Document Evidence. According to Chunk 2, high-value Procurement Controls require competitive supplier quotations. Additionally, procurement must verify budget availability before issuing the purchase order (Chunk 2). Furthermore, high-value purchases exceeding internal thresholds require a Request for Quotation (RFQ) to be issued to multiple suppliers (Chunk 3). 

Regarding purchase order approval, Chunk 5 states that purchase orders above 25,000 EUR require director-level approval.

Invoice Matching Policy
Purpose
This policy defines invoice matching procedures used by the ERP accounts payable module.
three-way Matching
Invoices are validated using a three-way match between purchase orders, goods receipts, and
supplier invoices.

Tolerance Rules

order.
Quantity variances may be tolerated up to 5 percent depending on product category.
Dispute Resolution
Invoices failing the matching process are flagged for review by the accounts payable team.

Invoice Matching Policy
Purpose
This policy defines invoice matching procedures used by the ERP accounts payable module.
three-way Matching

supplier invoices.

Tolerance Rules

order.

Dispute Resolution

This policy defines the payment terms, grace periods, discount rules, and late fee policies applied to
vendor and customer invoices in the ERP system. The goal is to ensure predictable financial
operations and consistent vendor relationships.
Standard Payment Terms
The standard payment term for invoices is Net 30 days from the invoice issue date.
Business units may request alternative payment terms for strategic vendors subject to finance
approval.
Payment schedules are automatically enforced through the ERP accounts payable module.
Grace Period

[Chunk 5 | text | Page 1 | Source purchase_order_approval_policy.pdf]
confirmation, delivery tracking, and invoice matching.
high-value Procurement Controls
high-value purchases require competitive supplier quotations.
Procurement must verify budget availability before issuing the purchase order.
Audit and Compliance
All purchase orders are logged in the ERP system and subject to internal audit review.

[Chunk 6 | text | Page 1 | Source payment_terms_policy.pdf]
Suppliers may offer early payment discounts, commonly expressed as 2/10 Net 30.
This indicates that a 2 percent discount is available if payment is made within 10 days.
The ERP system automatically identifies eligible discounts during invoice processing.
Exceptions and Special Agreements

[Chunk 7 | text | Page 1 | Source invoice_matching_policy.pdf]
Invoice Matching Policy
Purpose
This policy defines invoice matching procedures used by the ERP accounts payable module.
three-way Matching
Invoices are validated using a three-way match between purchase orders, goods receipts, and
supplier invoices.
This process ensures that invoices correspond to approved procurement transactions.
Tolerance Rules
A price variance tolerance of up to 2 percent may be allowed between the invoice and the purchase
order.
Quantity variances may be tolerated up to 5 percent depending on product category.
Dispute Resolution
Invoices failing the matching process are flagged for review by the accounts payable team.

Grace Period
A grace period of up to 5 calendar days after the invoice due date may be applied before late

This grace period accounts for banking delays, public holidays, and cross-border transactions.
Late Payment Fees
Invoices unpaid after the grace period may incur a late fee of 2% of the outstanding invoice amount.
Recurring overdue payments may trigger vendor review and procurement escalation.
Early Payment Discounts
Suppliers may offer early payment discounts, commonly expressed as 2/10 Net 30.

[Chunk 2 | text | Page 1 | Source invoice_matching_policy.pdf]
Invoice Matching Policy
Purpose
This policy defines invoice matching procedures used by the ERP accounts payable module.
three-way Matching
Invoices are validated using a three-way match between purchase orders, goods receipts, and
supplier invoices.
This process ensures that invoices correspond to approved procurement transactions.
Tolerance Rules
A price variance tolerance of up to 2 percent may be allowed between the invoice and the purchase
order.
Quantity variances may be tolerated up to 5 percent depending on product category.
Dispute Resolution
Invoices failing the matching process are flagged for review by the accounts payable team.

[Chunk 3 | text | Page 1 | Source payment_terms_policy.pdf]
Payment schedules are automatically enforced through the ERP accounts payable module.
Grace Period
A grace period of up to 5 calendar days after the invoice due date may be applied before late
penalties are enforced.
This grace period accounts for banking delays, public holidays, and cross-border transactions.
Late Payment Fees
Invoices unpaid after the grace period may incur a late fee of 2% of the outstanding invoice amount.
Recurring overdue payments may trigger vendor review and procurement escalation.
Early Payment Discounts
Suppliers may offer early payment discounts, commonly expressed as 2/10 Net 30.

Purpose
This policy defines the payment terms, grace periods, discount rules, and late fee policies applied to
vendor and customer invoices in the ERP system. The goal is to ensure predictable financial
operations and consistent vendor relationships.
Standard Payment Terms
The standard payment term for invoices is Net 30 days from the invoice issue date.
Business units may request alternative payment terms for strategic vendors subject to finance
approval.
Payment schedules are automatically enforced through the ERP accounts payable module.

document_output.answer:
The three-way matching process is used to validate invoices by comparing them with purchase orders and goods receipts. This process ensures that invoices correspond to approved procurement transactions. The three-way match is made between:

1. Purchase Orders: The original purchase order that was created to initiate the procurement process.
2. Goods Receipts: The goods receipt document that confirms the receipt of goods from the supplier.
3. Supplier Invoices: The invoice submitted by the supplier for payment.

This process helps to prevent incorrect or fraudulent invoices from being paid.

However, the information on product categories with the lowest margins is not available in the provided documents.

query: What procurement governance rules exist and what is the total purchase order value by vendor?

final_response.answer:
Procurement governance rules include:
- Evaluating suppliers based on cost, quality, delivery reliability, and compliance certifications.
- Competitive bidding is required for high-value procurements.
- Request for Quotation (RFQ) must be issued to multiple suppliers for purchases exceeding internal thresholds.
- Procurement must verify budget availability before issuing the purchase order.
- All procurement activities must comply with company ethical standards and anti-corruption policies.
- Employees involved in procurement decisions must disclose conflicts of interest.
- Purchase orders below 5,000 EUR may be approved by department managers, between 5,000 EUR and 25,000 EUR require procurement manager approval, and above 25,000 EUR require director-level approval.
- The ERP system manages the full purchase order lifecycle including creation, approval, supplier confirmation, delivery tracking, and invoice matching.
- Certain strategic vendors may negotiate custom payment terms such as Net 45 or Net 60, which must be recorded in the vendor master record and approved by the finance department.
- Invoices are validated using a three-way match between purchase orders, goods receipts, and supplier invoices.
- A price variance tolerance of up to 2 percent may be allowed between the invoice and the purchase order.
- Quantity variances may be tolerated up to 5 percent depending on product category.
- Vendors must submit a completed vendor registration form, tax identification documents, and bank account details to be onboarded into the ERP procurement system.
final_response.reasoning: The procurement governance rules are outlined in the Procurement Governance Guidelines document (Chunk 1 and Chunk 4). The Purchase Order Approval Policy (Chunk 5 and Chunk 10) defines the approval process for purchase orders based on value. The Invoice Matching Policy (Chunk 7) describes the three-way matching process for invoices. The Vendor Onboarding Policy (Chunk 8) outlines the required documentation and approval workflow for new vendors. The Payment Terms Policy (Chunk 6 and Chunk 9) explains the custom payment terms that may be negotiated with certain strategic vendors.

Invoice Matching Policy
Purpose
This policy defines invoice matching procedures used by the ERP accounts payable module.
three-way Matching
Invoices are validated using a three-way match between purchase orders, goods receipts, and
supplier invoices.
This process ensures that invoices correspond to approved procurement transactions.
Tolerance Rules
A price variance tolerance of up to 2 percent may be allowed between the invoice and the purchase
order.
Quantity variances may be tolerated up to 5 percent depending on product category.
Dispute Resolution
Invoices failing the matching process are flagged for review by the accounts payable team.

[Chunk 8 | text | Page 1 | Source vendor_onboarding_policy.pdf]
Vendor Onboarding Policy
Purpose
This document defines the standard procedure for onboarding new vendors into the ERP
procurement system.
Required Vendor Documentation
Vendors must submit a completed vendor registration form.
Tax identification documents must be provided for regulatory compliance.
Bank account details must be verified to prevent fraudulent payments.
Compliance certifications such as ISO or regulatory licenses may be required depending on vendor
category.
Vendor Approval Workflow
Vendor onboarding follows a three-stage approval workflow: procurement review, finance
validation, and compliance verification.

Procurement governance rules include:
- Evaluating suppliers based on cost, quality, delivery reliability, and compliance certifications.
- Competitive bidding is required for high-value procurements.
- Request for Quotation (RFQ) must be issued to multiple suppliers for purchases exceeding internal thresholds.
- Procurement must verify budget availability before issuing the purchase order.
- All procurement activities must comply with company ethical standards and anti-corruption policies.
- Employees involved in procurement decisions must disclose conflicts of interest.
- Purchase orders below 5,000 EUR may be approved by department managers, between 5,000 EUR and 25,000 EUR require procurement manager approval, and above 25,000 EUR require director-level approval.
- The ERP system manages the full purchase order lifecycle including creation, approval, supplier confirmation, delivery tracking, and invoice matching.
- Certain strategic vendors may negotiate custom payment terms such as Net 45 or Net 60, which must be recorded in the vendor master record and approved by the finance department.
- Invoices are validated using a three-way match between purchase orders, goods receipts, and supplier invoices.
- A price variance tolerance of up to 2 percent may be allowed between the invoice and the purchase order.
- Quantity variances may be tolerated up to 5 percent depending on product category.
- Vendors must submit a completed vendor registration form, tax identification documents, and bank account details to be onboarded into the ERP procurement system.

[Chunk 4 | text | Page 1 | Source payment_terms_policy.pdf]
ERP Payment Terms Policy
Purpose
This policy defines the payment terms, grace periods, discount rules, and late fee policies applied to
vendor and customer invoices in the ERP system. The goal is to ensure predictable financial
operations and consistent vendor relationships.
Standard Payment Terms
The standard payment term for invoices is Net 30 days from the invoice issue date.
Business units may request alternative payment terms for strategic vendors subject to finance
approval.
Payment schedules are automatically enforced through the ERP accounts payable module.
Grace Period

[Chunk 6 | text | Page 1 | Source payment_terms_policy.pdf]
Suppliers may offer early payment discounts, commonly expressed as 2/10 Net 30.
This indicates that a 2 percent discount is available if payment is made within 10 days.
The ERP system automatically identifies eligible discounts during invoice processing.
Exceptions and Special Agreements

[Chunk 7 | text | Page 1 | Source purchase_order_approval_policy.pdf]
confirmation, delivery tracking, and invoice matching.
high-value Procurement Controls
high-value purchases require competitive supplier quotations.
Procurement must verify budget availability before issuing the purchase order.
Audit and Compliance
All purchase orders are logged in the ERP system and subject to internal audit review.

[Chunk 8 | text | Page 1 | Source invoice_matching_policy.pdf]
Invoice Matching Policy
Purpose
This policy defines invoice matching procedures used by the ERP accounts payable module.
three-way Matching
Invoices are validated using a three-way match between purchase orders, goods receipts, and
supplier invoices.
This process ensures that invoices correspond to approved procurement transactions.
Tolerance Rules
A price variance tolerance of up to 2 percent may be allowed between the invoice and the purchase
order.
Quantity variances may be tolerated up to 5 percent depending on product category.
Dispute Resolution
Invoices failing the matching process are flagged for review by the accounts payable team.

[Chunk 9 | text | Page 1 | Source payment_terms_policy.pdf]
Payment schedules are automatically enforced through the ERP accounts payable module.
Grace Period
A grace period of up to 5 calendar days after the invoice due date may be applied before late
penalties are enforced.
This grace period accounts for banking delays, public holidays, and cross-border transactions.
Late Payment Fees
Invoices unpaid after the grace period may incur a late fee of 2% of the outstanding invoice amount.
Recurring overdue payments may trigger vendor review and procurement escalation.
Early Payment Discounts
Suppliers may offer early payment discounts, commonly expressed as 2/10 Net 30.

[Chunk 10 | text | Page 1 | Source purchase_order_approval_policy.pdf]
Purchase Order Approval Policy
Purpose
This document defines the purchase order approval process used within the ERP procurement
module.
Purchase Order Thresholds
Purchase orders below 5,000 EUR may be approved by department managers.
Purchase orders between 5,000 EUR and 25,000 EUR require procurement manager approval.
Purchase orders above 25,000 EUR require director-level approval.
Purchase Order Lifecycle
The ERP system manages the full purchase order lifecycle including creation, approval, supplier
confirmation, delivery tracking, and invoice matching.

[Chunk 7 | text | Page 2 | Source payment_terms_policy.pdf]
Source: payment_terms_policy.pdf | Page: 2 | Type: text

Certain strategic vendors may negotiate custom payment terms such as Net 45 or Net 60.

[Chunk 8 | text | Page 1 | Source invoice_matching_policy.pdf]
Invoice Matching Policy
Purpose
This policy defines invoice matching procedures used by the ERP accounts payable module.
three-way Matching
Invoices are validated using a three-way match between purchase orders, goods receipts, and
supplier invoices.
This process ensures that invoices correspond to approved procurement transactions.
Tolerance Rules
A price variance tolerance of up to 2 percent may be allowed between the invoice and the purchase
order.
Quantity variances may be tolerated up to 5 percent depending on product category.
Dispute Resolution
Invoices failing the matching process are flagged for review by the accounts payable team.

validation, and compliance verification.

Vendor Master Data Management
All vendor information is stored in the vendor master table of the ERP system.
Changes to vendor bank details require dual approval.

[Chunk 11 | text | Page 1 | Source payment_terms_policy.pdf]
Payment schedules are automatically enforced through the ERP accounts payable module.
Grace Period
A grace period of up to 5 calendar days after the invoice due date may be applied before late
penalties are enforced.
This grace period accounts for banking delays, public holidays, and cross-border transactions.
Late Payment Fees
Invoices unpaid after the grace period may incur a late fee of 2% of the outstanding invoice amount.
Recurring overdue payments may trigger vendor review and procurement escalation.
Early Payment Discounts
Suppliers may offer early payment discounts, commonly expressed as 2/10 Net 30.

Purpose
This document defines the purchase order approval process used within the ERP procurement

Purchase Order Thresholds
Purchase orders below 5,000 EUR may be approved by department managers.
Purchase orders between 5,000 EUR and 25,000 EUR require procurement manager approval.
Purchase orders above 25,000 EUR require director-level approval.
Purchase Order Lifecycle
The ERP system manages the full purchase order lifecycle including creation, approval, supplier
confirmation, delivery tracking, and invoice matching.
high-value Procurement Controls

Purpose
This document defines the standard procedure for onboarding new vendors into the ERP
procurement system.
Required Vendor Documentation
Vendors must submit a completed vendor registration form.
Tax identification documents must be provided for regulatory compliance.
Bank account details must be verified to prevent fraudulent payments.
Compliance certifications such as ISO or regulatory licenses may be required depending on vendor
category.
Vendor Approval Workflow
Vendor onboarding follows a three-stage approval workflow: procurement review, finance
validation, and compliance verification.

compliance certifications.
Competitive bidding is required for high-value procurements.
Request for Quotation Process
For purchases exceeding internal thresholds, a Request for Quotation (RFQ) must be issued to
multiple suppliers.

[Chunk 14 | text | Page 1 | Source invoice_matching_policy.pdf]
Invoice Matching Policy
Purpose
This policy defines invoice matching procedures used by the ERP accounts payable module.
three-way Matching
Invoices are validated using a three-way match between purchase orders, goods receipts, and
supplier invoices.
This process ensures that invoices correspond to approved procurement transactions.
Tolerance Rules
A price variance tolerance of up to 2 percent may be allowed between the invoice and the purchase
order.
Quantity variances may be tolerated up to 5 percent depending on product category.
Dispute Resolution
Invoices failing the matching process are flagged for review by the accounts payable team.

Vendor Onboarding Policy
Purpose
This document defines the standard procedure for onboarding new vendors into the ERP
procurement system.
Required Vendor Documentation
Vendors must submit a completed vendor registration form.
Tax identification documents must be provided for regulatory compliance.
Bank account details must be verified to prevent fraudulent payments.
Compliance certifications such as ISO or regulatory licenses may be required depending on vendor
category.
Vendor Approval Workflow
Vendor onboarding follows a three-stage approval workflow: procurement review, finance
validation, and compliance verification.

Purchase Order Approval Policy
Purpose
This document defines the purchase order approval process used within the ERP procurement
module.
Purchase Order Thresholds
Purchase orders below 5,000 EUR may be approved by department managers.
Purchase orders between 5,000 EUR and 25,000 EUR require procurement manager approval.
Purchase orders above 25,000 EUR require director-level approval.
Purchase Order Lifecycle
The ERP system manages the full purchase order lifecycle including creation, approval, supplier
confirmation, delivery tracking, and invoice matching.
high-value Procurement Controls

three-way Matching

supplier invoices.

Tolerance Rules
A price variance tolerance of up to 2 percent may be allowed between the invoice and the purchase
order.

Dispute Resolution

ERP Payment Terms Policy
Purpose
This policy defines the payment terms, grace periods, discount rules, and late fee policies applied to
vendor and customer invoices in the ERP system. The goal is to ensure predictable financial

Standard Payment Terms

approval.

Grace Period

[Chunk 1 | text | Page 1 | Source invoice_matching_policy.pdf]
Invoices failing the matching process are flagged for review by the accounts payable team.

Automated Controls
The ERP system automatically flags mismatches exceeding tolerance thresholds and prevents

[Chunk 2 | text | Page 1 | Source invoice_matching_policy.pdf]
Invoice Matching Policy
Purpose
This policy defines invoice matching procedures used by the ERP accounts payable module.
three-way Matching
Invoices are validated using a three-way match between purchase orders, goods receipts, and
supplier invoices.
This process ensures that invoices correspond to approved procurement transactions.
Tolerance Rules
A price variance tolerance of up to 2 percent may be allowed between the invoice and the purchase
order.
Quantity variances may be tolerated up to 5 percent depending on product category.
Dispute Resolution
Invoices failing the matching process are flagged for review by the accounts payable team.

[Chunk 3 | text | Page 1 | Source invoice_matching_policy.pdf]
Source: invoice_matching_policy.pdf | Page: 1 | Type: text | Section: Invoice Matching Policy

[Chunk 4 | text | Page 1 | Source payment_terms_policy.pdf]
Payment schedules are automatically enforced through the ERP accounts payable module.
Grace Period
A grace period of up to 5 calendar days after the invoice due date may be applied before late
penalties are enforced.
This grace period accounts for banking delays, public holidays, and cross-border transactions.
Late Payment Fees
Invoices unpaid after the grace period may incur a late fee of 2% of the outstanding invoice amount.
Recurring overdue payments may trigger vendor review and procurement escalation.
Early Payment Discounts
Suppliers may offer early payment discounts, commonly expressed as 2/10 Net 30.

[Chunk 5 | text | Page 1 | Source payment_terms_policy.pdf]
ERP Payment Terms Policy
Purpose
This policy defines the payment terms, grace periods, discount rules, and late fee policies applied to
vendor and customer invoices in the ERP system. The goal is to ensure predictable financial
operations and consistent vendor relationships.
Standard Payment Terms
The standard payment term for invoices is Net 30 days from the invoice issue date.
Business units may request alternative payment terms for strategic vendors subject to finance
approval.
Payment schedules are automatically enforced through the ERP accounts payable module.
Grace Period

[Chunk 6 | text | Page 1 | Source purchase_order_approval_policy.pdf]
confirmation, delivery tracking, and invoice matching.
high-value Procurement Controls
high-value purchases require competitive supplier quotations.
Procurement must verify budget availability before issuing the purchase order.
Audit and Compliance
All purchase orders are logged in the ERP system and subject to internal audit review.

[Chunk 7 | text | Page 1 | Source payment_terms_policy.pdf]
Suppliers may offer early payment discounts, commonly expressed as 2/10 Net 30.
This indicates that a 2 percent discount is available if payment is made within 10 days.
The ERP system automatically identifies eligible discounts during invoice processing.
Exceptions and Special Agreements

[Chunk 8 | text | Page 1 | Source purchase_order_approval_policy.pdf]
Source: purchase_order_approval_policy.pdf | Page: 1 | Type: text | Section: Purchase Order Approval Policy

[Chunk 9 | text | Page 1 | Source purchase_order_approval_policy.pdf]
Purchase Order Approval Policy
Purpose
This document defines the purchase order approval process used within the ERP procurement
module.
Purchase Order Thresholds
Purchase orders below 5,000 EUR may be approved by department managers.
Purchase orders between 5,000 EUR and 25,000 EUR require procurement manager approval.
Purchase orders above 25,000 EUR require director-level approval.
Purchase Order Lifecycle
The ERP system manages the full purchase order lifecycle including creation, approval, supplier
confirmation, delivery tracking, and invoice matching.
high-value Procurement Controls

[Chunk 10 | text | Page 2 | Source payment_terms_policy.pdf]
Source: payment_terms_policy.pdf | Page: 2 | Type: text

Certain strategic vendors may negotiate custom payment terms such as Net 45 or Net 60.
Such agreements must be recorded in the vendor master record and approved by the finance
department.
